articles.title: Welcome to the Summer of Jimothy! Here’s Why We’re All Talking About One Very Unusual Raccoon in Seattle
articles.content:
有时我觉得浣熊在跟踪我。我说的不只是我每周都会看到的那些吃得圆滚滚的家伙——它们在我家附近蹒跚散步，探头探脑地越过我家篱笆，偶尔还会在我的泳池里凉快一下。不，这些是“专业”浣熊，就是我需要写的那种。就在几周前，我了解到浣熊可能在驯化自己，而这个周末，又一个故事落到了我手上：吉姆西，小浣熊的逆袭之路。

吉姆西是一只来自西雅图的可爱但形状奇特的浣熊，它已经走进了我们的内心和社交媒体——而且，就像我住处的浣熊朋友们一样，它似乎没有任何要离开的迹象。它与其他浣熊有点不同，人们似乎对吉姆西奇怪的小身体和活泼的态度百看不厌。

那么，为什么这场最新的动物痴迷占据了我们的生活呢？我的意思是，除了显而易见的答案——它绝对可爱之外。继续往下看，看看吉姆西在做什么，以及为什么我们完全被它的小把戏所吸引。

上周，西雅图的基安娜·霍尔在Instagram上发布了一段视频，视频中一只长相奇特但脚步轻盈的浣熊穿过公园，蹦跳着上了一段楼梯。那天是她的结婚纪念日，她和伴侣外出散步时发现了这个形状奇特的生物，他们最初以为是一只猫。（“我在看什么？”可以从背景中听到霍尔的声音。）

就像我们现在常做的那样，霍尔掏出手机，捕捉到了继“打猴子”之后最可爱的野生动物镜头，并给它取名为吉姆西。“它看起来就像个吉姆西，”她后来说。当然，我们可以接受这个说法。（嘿，当你想到一个传遍世界的浣熊名字时，你可以选一个不同的。）

根据华盛顿州立大学兽医教学医院的副教授玛西·洛格斯顿博士的说法，吉姆西的畸形似乎是一种先天性疾病（如短脊柱综合症）的结果——不过她说，如果不进行检查就无法确定。

短脊柱综合症发生在子宫内，当某些椎骨融合时，会导致受影响的动物颈部和背部非常短。但我们这位新朋友似乎在各方面都做得很好。“问题是，这只动物能否找到食物，它们能否活动？”洛格斯顿博士告诉《纽约邮报》。“吉姆西似乎这两件事都做得相当好。”太好了！

“我很惊讶，说实话也有点受启发，它竟然这么有韧性，”她告诉《西雅图时报》。事实上，西雅图居民本·特拉梅尔发布了一段视频，据称是婴儿时期的吉姆西，在他成名之前拍摄的，从中可以看出它长大了多少。特拉梅尔还描述了它的妈妈和兄弟姐妹在小吉姆西还是幼崽时如何帮助它跟上步伐。太可爱了。

现在是2026年，我们都需要生活中的一些轻松时刻！

“对每个人来说都很艰难，”霍尔谈到她随意拍摄的视频时说。“每个人都需要一些愚蠢有趣的东西来调剂心情。”即使我们没有战争肆虐、油价飙升和即将到来的两极分化的中期选举，一个关于一只顽强的小劣势者的温馨故事总是受欢迎的。尤其是在这个奇怪而奇妙的互联网上。

“我从未想过我的浣熊视频会走红，”霍尔说。“我很高兴它能为人们带来某种快乐。”

怎么会没有呢，这才是更好的问题。吉姆西的家乡西雅图历来以某种酷劲接纳古怪和被遗弃的事物，现在已经全面投入了“我的天啊”的狂热欢呼。当地人在发布目击照片和视频，创作壁画，分享粉丝艺术，甚至纹上这位夏季明星的纹身。

7月26日周日，这座城市将在最初发现吉姆西的公园附近发布官方公告纪念“吉姆西之夏”。（带上你的创意作品来参加“友好艺术竞赛”吧！）华盛顿大学授予了“吉姆西博士”荣誉博士学位，西雅图水手队也参与其中，在社交媒体上发布了一张“戴着”水手队棒球帽的小吉姆西的照片。

当地人和网民称吉姆西是“最具西雅图特色的动物”，但我认为我们都可以用一点“热辣吉姆西夏天”来形容此刻（向梅根·斯泰里奥恩致敬）。正如一位r/JimothyTheRaccoon子版块的Reddit用户所说：“我们需要一个能团结我们的东西。吉姆西响应了号召🦝。”
articles.summary:
Sometimes I feel like raccoons are following me. And I’m not just talking about my near-weekly sightings of the well-fed fellows who toddle around my neighborhood, peek over my fence and occasionally cool off in my pool. No, these are “professional” raccoons, aka ones that I get to write about. Just a few weeks ago, I learned that raccoons could be domesticating themselves, and this weekend, another fell into my lap: Jimothy, The Little Raccoon That Could.

Jimothy, a delightful, if strangely shaped, raccoon from Seattle, has moved into our hearts and social media feeds—and, like my resident raccoon friends, shows no signs of vacating anytime soon. He’s a little different from other raccoons, and people can’t seem to get enough of Jimothy’s odd little body and plucky attitude.

So why has this latest animal obsession taken over our lives? I mean, besides the obvious answer, which is that he is absolutely adorable. Read on to see what Jimothy’s been up to and why we are completely riveted by his antics.

Get Reader’s Digest’s Read Up newsletter for more news, animals, humor, travel, tech and fun facts all week long.

How did Jimothy come into our lives?

View this post on Instagram

A post shared by Kiana (@c1vrgr1)


Last week, Seattle’s Kiana Hall posted a video on Instagram of an odd-looking yet fleet-footed raccoon bounding across a park and up a flight of steps. It was her anniversary, and she and her partner were out for a walk when they spied the strangely shaped creature, which they first mistook for a cat. (“What am I looking at?” Hall can be heard saying in the background.)

As we do these days, Hall whipped out her phone, captured the cutest wildlife shot since Punch the Monkey and named him Jimothy. “He just looked like a Jimothy to me,” she said later. Sure, we can accept that. (Hey, when you come up with a raccoon name heard ’round the world, you can pick a different one.)

Why does he look like that—and is he OK?

Jimothy’s deformity appears to be the result of a congenital condition such as short spine syndrome, according to Dr. Marcie Logsdon, an associate professor at Washington State University’s Veterinary Teaching Hospital—though she says it’s impossible to know for sure without an examination.

Short spine syndrome occurs in utero when certain vertebrae fuse, leaving affected animals with a very short neck and back. But our new friend seems to be doing well, all things considered. “The question is, is the animal able to find food, and are they able to move?” Dr. Logsdon told the New York Post. “Jimothy seems to do both of those things reasonably well.” Phew!

“I was surprised and honestly a little bit inspired that he’s that resilient,” she told the Seattle Times. In fact, Seattle resident Ben Trammell posted a video of what appears to be Baby Jimothy, taken prior to his viral fame, and it’s clear just how much he’s grown. Trammell also described how much his mom and siblings helped him keep up when he was little. Awww.

Why is the internet obsessed?

It’s 2026 and we all need some lightness in our lives, that’s why!

“Times are hard for everyone,” Hall says of the video she shot on a lark. “Everyone could use something silly and fun like this.” And even if we didn’t have wars raging, gas prices soaring and polarizing midterm elections right around the corner, a feel-good story involving a scrappy little underdog is always welcome. Especially on the weird and wonderful internet.

“I never thought that my video of a raccoon would take off,” Hall says. “I’m really happy that he could bring some kind of joy to people.”

How has it become the summer of Jimothy?

How has it not, is the better question. Jimothy’s hometown of Seattle, which has a long history of embracing the odd and outcast with a certain coolness, has gone all-in on the OMG squeeing. Locals are posting pictures and videos of sightings, creating murals, sharing fan art and even getting tattoos of the summer’s breakout star.

On Sunday, July 26, the city will commemorate Jimothy Summer by issuing an official proclamation at a park near where he was originally sighted. (Bring your creative endeavors for a “friendly art competition”!) The University of Washington gave “Dr. Jimothy” an honorary doctorate, and the Seattle Mariners also got into the act, posting a picture on social media of Baby Jimothy “wearing” a Mariners ball cap.

Locals and the internet are calling Jimothy “the most Seattle animal possible,” but I think we can all use a little Hot Jimothy Summer right now (with a nod to Megan Thee Stallion). As a Redditor on the r/JimothyTheRaccoon subreddit said, “We needed something to unite us. Jimothy answered the call 🩷🦝.”

Sources:

  • Today: “Seattle Darling Jimothy the Raccoon Has Scampered into Hearts Across the Globe”
  • Australian Broadcasting Corporation: “‘Jimothy,’ a raccoon with short spine syndrome who roams Seattle, gains cult following online”
  • People: “Oddly-Shaped Raccoon Believed to Have a Spine Deformity Becomes Seattle’s Newest Viral Star”
  • UPI: “Seattle’s round raccoon Jimothy becomes viral star”
  • New York Post: “The ‘sad’ truth about Jimothy, Seattle’s uber-viral mutant raccoon with a short spine”
  • Reddit: “r/JimothyTheRaccoon”

The post Welcome to the Summer of Jimothy! Here’s Why We’re All Talking About One Very Unusual Raccoon in Seattle appeared first on Reader's Digest.

articles.title: This Is the Sweet—And Oh-So-Royal—Way Queen Elizabeth Indulged Prince William’s Love of Dessert
articles.content:
伊丽莎白女王与威廉王子之间有着特殊的感情，在很多方面，他们就像普通的祖孙一样。但我们也不要自欺欺人：他们毕竟是皇室成员，这意味着“宠孙子”的方式肯定是非同寻常的。至少，在威廉最喜欢的甜点这件事上确实如此。至少，女王的前厨师达伦·麦克格雷迪的说法确实如此，当年轻的王子想要他喜欢的甜食时，女王有一种神奇的方式来满足他。可以说，我们完全进入了威利·旺卡巧克力工厂的世界。

那么威廉王子小时候渴望什么，他的慈爱祖母又是如何满足他的呢？继续往下读吧。

按一下按钮！是的，这对任何孩子来说都是梦想成真：按一下按钮，冰淇淋就出现了——而且还是由戴着手套的男仆端上来的！据麦克格雷迪说，事情就是这样。他曾在女王手下工作，后来转到戴安娜王妃的府邸工作。

“我为女王工作时，威廉王子喜欢黑巧克力，他想要巧克力冰淇淋，”麦克格雷迪告诉英国Heart Bingo节目。“女王会按一下按钮，侍从就会过来，她会要求冰淇淋，然后触发一长串程序：行政总厨、糕点师、银器室、玻璃器皿室、亚麻室。最后，20分钟后，威廉得到了他的自制冰淇淋。”

希望沿着铺着红毯的长廊走这么长的路，冰淇淋到的时候不会已经化了！

我们不确定，但我们猜至少有时候是这样。毕竟，已故女王也超爱冰淇淋——她甚至在1947年的婚礼招待会上供应冰淇淋。宾客们享用了“伊丽莎白公主冰淇淋球”，也就是草莓冰淇淋做成圆顶形状。麦克格雷迪说，女王经常在宴会上供应这种甜点的变种，比如咖啡味的“科普莉亚冰淇淋球”。

和威廉一样，女王也喜欢黑巧克力，而且这种喜爱不仅限于冰淇淋。她 former lady-in-waiting Lady Pamela Hicks 在纪录片《我与女王共度的岁月》中透露，这位君主总是随身带着一盒巧克力，藏在她的房间里，这样她“贪吃”的家人就不会在她之前吃掉！

她最喜欢的是高级巧克力商Charbonnel et Walker的巧克力和果仁糖，以及Bendicks的巧克力薄荷糖。事实上，她太喜欢这些零食了，以至于授予了这两个品牌皇家认证，这意味着它们被法律允许在产品上展示皇家徽章。

不。麦克格雷迪透露，戴安娜在肯辛顿宫的厨房环境更加随意。那里没有神奇的冰淇淋按钮——更像是自己打开冰箱。“在肯辛顿宫，气氛轻松多了，”他说。“威廉会走进厨房说，'达伦，请给我一些巧克力冰淇淋好吗？'我会说，'请自便。'”

据这位厨师说，威廉会“拿起哈根达斯巧克力脆片——那是他最喜欢的——打开盖子，坐在窗台上吃。”他补充说，戴安娜也喜欢在厨房里闲逛、吃点零食、聊聊天。

他现在肯定还是这样。至今他仍然是巧克力冰淇淋的忠实粉丝。他和凯特经常被拍到享用冰淇淋，比如2020年两人访问威尔士斯旺西的一家冰淇淋店时。“我是个巧克力控，”他告诉乔伊冰淇淋店的店主。

同样，在2022年威廉和凯特的加勒比之旅中，他们访问了伯利兹的一家巧克力农场。在那里，威廉开玩笑地问是否可以找一份学徒的工作，说：“这很适合我。”

威廉在附近的伊顿公学读书时——他刚在那里为乔治王子办理了入学——他会去温莎城堡和祖母一起喝下午茶。这位王子在最近一段视频致敬中透露了那些下午对他来说意义重大，他说：“我记得很多个安静的下午，在城堡里和她还有祖父一起喝茶、聊天、分享故事，看到她在这里是多么自在，被家人和她心爱的狗和马围绕。直到今天，她给了我一种我从未知道自己需要的下午茶情结。”

威廉还用他和凯特2011年婚礼上的蛋糕选择向她表达了特别的敬意。除了传统的婚礼水果蛋糕，威廉还准备了第二种蛋糕——巧克力脆饼蛋糕，那是女王最喜欢的。麦克格雷迪告诉《今日美食》，这款蛋糕是“她最喜欢的蛋糕”，如果它在白金汉宫有剩余，就会被“送到温莎城堡，这样她就可以在那里把它吃完。我以前经常带着装着脆饼蛋糕的铁盒子从伦敦坐火车去温莎城堡。蛋糕已经被吃了一半了。”
articles.summary:
Queen Elizabeth and Prince William had a special connection, and in many ways, they were just like a regular grandma and grandson. But let’s not kid ourselves: They were still royals, which means the idea of “spoiling your grandkids” was likely next-level. At least, that was definitely the case when it came to William’s favorite dessert.

According to the queen’s ex-chef Darren McGrady, when the young prince wanted his preferred sweet treat, the queen had a magical way of indulging him. And let’s just say we are totally getting into Willy Wonka territory here.

So what did Prince William crave as a kid, and how did his doting grandmother fulfill those wishes? Read on to find out.

Get Reader’s Digest’s Read Up newsletter for more royals, humor, cleaning, travel, tech and fun facts all week long.

How did Queen Elizabeth indulge Prince William’s love of dessert?

With the push of a button! Yes, it’s the stuff of dreams for any child: You press a button and ice cream appears—served by a gloved footman, no less! And that’s exactly what happened, according to McGrady, who worked for the queen before transitioning to a job in Princess Diana’s household.

“When I worked for the queen, Prince William loved dark chocolate and asked for chocolate ice cream,” McGrady told the U.K.’s Heart Bingo. “The queen would press a button, the page would come, she’d ask for the ice cream, and it triggered a long chain: head chef, pastry chef, silver pantry, glass pantry, linen room. Finally, 20 minutes later, William got his homemade ice cream presented to him.”

Let’s hope the long trot along the red-carpeted corridors didn’t mean the ice cream melted by the time he got it!

Did the queen join her grandson for ice cream?

We don’t know for sure, but we’re guessing she did at least some of the time. After all, the late queen loved ice cream too—so much so that she served it as part of her wedding reception in 1947. Guests tucked into Bombe Glacée Princess Elizabeth, aka strawberry ice cream in a dome. McGrady said that the queen regularly included variations of this at her banquets, such as the coffee-flavored Bombe Glacée Coppelia.

Like William, the queen adored dark chocolate, and that love wasn’t limited to ice cream. Her former lady-in-waiting, Lady Pamela Hicks, revealed on the documentary My Years with the Queen that the monarch always had a box of chocolates with her, which she hid in her room so her “greedy” family wouldn’t eat them before she did!

Her favorites were chocolates and pralines from upmarket chocolatier Charbonnel et Walker and chocolate mints from Bendicks. In fact, she enjoyed these treats so much that she awarded both brands royal warrants, meaning they were legally allowed to display the royal coat of arms on their offerings.

Was Diana stricter with William’s dessert intake?

Nope. Diana ran an even more casual kitchen environment at Kensington Palace, McGrady revealed. There was no magic ice cream button there—it was more a case of opening the freezer yourself. “At Kensington, it was much more relaxed,” he said. “William would walk into the kitchen and say, ‘Darren, can I have some chocolate ice cream, please?’ I’d say, ‘Help yourself.'”

According to the chef, William would “grab Häagen-Dazs chocolate chip—that was his favorite—open it, and sit in the windowsill eating it.” He added that Diana also liked to hang out in the kitchen, have a snack and catch up.

Does William still have a sweet tooth?

He sure does. To this day, he’s still a chocolate ice cream fan. He and Kate are often snapped enjoying ice cream, such as when the duo visited an ice cream parlor in Swansea, Wales, in 2020. “I am a chocolate man,” he told the owners of Joe’s Ice Cream Parlor.

Likewise, during William and Kate’s Caribbean tour in 2022, they visited a chocolate farm in Belize. While there, William jokingly asked if he could get a job as an apprentice, saying, “It’s my kind of thing.”

How else did Elizabeth and William bond?

When William was attending school at nearby Eton College—where he’s just enrolled Prince George—he would spend Sundays having tea with his grandmother at Windsor Castle. The prince revealed just how much those afternoons meant to him in a recent video tribute, saying, “I remember many a quiet afternoon, having tea with her and my grandfather at the castle, chatting, sharing stories and seeing how completely at ease she was here, surrounded by family and her much-loved dogs and horses. To this day, she’s given me a love of teatime that I never knew I needed.”

William also paid a special tribute to her with his and Kate’s choice of cake at their 2011 wedding. As well as a traditional fruit cake, William had a second cake—a chocolate biscuit cake, something the queen adored. McGrady told Today Food that the cake was “her favorite cake” and that if anything was left when she had it at Buckingham Palace, it then went “to Windsor Castle so she [could] finish it there. I used to travel on the train from London to Windsor Castle with the biscuit cake in a tin on my knee. It was half-eaten.”

Sources:

  • Marie Claire: “Princess Diana’s Former Chef Shares How Queen Elizabeth Would Indulge Prince William with One Treat by ‘Pressing a Button’”
  • Royal.uk: “70 Facts about the Queen and Duke of Edinburgh’s wedding”
  • My Years with the Queen
  • The Standard: “Kate Middleton and Prince William tuck into ice-cream scoops during Swansea visit”
  • BBC: “William and Kate dance and taste chocolate during day two of Belize tour”
  • The Royal Chef: “Queen Elizabeth’s Favorite Cake: Chocolate Biscuit Cake”

The post This Is the Sweet—And Oh-So-Royal—Way Queen Elizabeth Indulged Prince William’s Love of Dessert appeared first on Reader's Digest.
